entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'Memory Space
Allow Kimi to save your preferences long-term and use your past conversations to improve future chats
Memory
100% used
User has successfully implemented full Keep/Undo workflow for agentic file operations in RawrXD Agentic IDE with production-ready implementation: 114-line header, 300+ line implementation, CMake integration, 50-item undo stack, dark theme UI, and complete error handling. Build status: compilation-ready, waiting for MainWindow_v5.cpp wiring and AgenticExecutor integration.
User has successfully fixed moc_includes.cpp include path and restored agent_chat_breadcrumb.cpp with all enterprise features. Build is now clean for breadcrumb component with HTML-rich tooltips, async Ollama fetching, metadata extraction, and Qt6 compatibility. Ready for comprehensive production enhancement phase.
User wants fully continued enterprise production-ready implementation with comprehensive error handling, monitoring, and advanced features for the streaming GGUF system.
User wants lazy loading/streaming GGUF implementation that dynamically manages RAM usage by loading/unloading as needed, staying within 64GB limit to run large models.
User wants full enhancement performance model implementation with comprehensive performance optimization, monitoring, and advanced model management features to complement the experimental features menu.
User wants fully coded, enterprise production-ready menu toggles for experimental features (not stubs), with real implementations for dynamic quantization, KV cache optimization, speculative decoding, and streaming model loading.
User wants experimental features for running larger models on lower-end systems, including advanced quantization, model compression, speculative decoding, KV cache optimization, and memory-efficient inference techniques.
User wants the complete 44-tool enterprise implementation provided as a downloadable archive rather than inline code. They want the full production-grade codebase packaged for download.
User wants complete, full source code implementation of all 44 VS Code Copilot tools (22 Built-in + 22 GitHub PR) in a single comprehensive codebase, with production-grade enterprise implementations including security, error handling, and observability.
User wants to implement ALL 44 VS Code Copilot tools (22 Built-in + 22 GitHub PR) in their enterprise AI IDE, with production-grade implementations, not placeholders. They want comprehensive implementation covering all tool categories simultaneously.
User wants to implement enterprise-grade, non-placeholding tools for their AI IDE based on VS Code Copilot's 44-tool ecosystem (22 Built-in + 22 GitHub PR tools). They have working inference pipeline but need real tool implementations with proper security, error handling, and production observability.
User has conducted comprehensive IDE audit revealing numerous unwired/incomplete areas across Core Application Logic, UI/Interaction, Networking/Security, GPU/Backend, and Observability. Critical issues include: missing JWT validation, WebView2 integration, command execution wiring, Vulkan initialization, streaming inference, and production observability (logging/metrics/tracing). Next priority is systematic implementation of core functionality before production readiness.
Deployment package completed successfully: RawrXD-v1.0.0-win64.zip (13.9 MB) contains full IDE suite with dual launchers (batch + PowerShell), complete documentation, Qt runtime, and organized structure. Package includes RawrXD-Win32IDE.exe (2.2MB), RawrXD-AgenticIDE.exe (2.0MB), RawrXD-Agent.exe (168KB CLI), plus benchmarking tools. Models folder ready for user GGUF files. Size comparison: Core executables 6.6MB + Qt runtime 24.8MB = professional distribution package. Status: READY FOR RELEASE.
Phi-3-mini benchmark completed with outstanding scaling results: 2.3GB model (3.6x larger) achieved 3,158 TPS (38% of TinyLlama performance) vs expected 28% linear scaling - 35% better than predicted. Sub-millisecond latency maintained at 0.32ms. Competitive analysis: RawrXD+Phi-3 (3,158 TPS, 3.8B params, <1ms first token) vs Cursor (~2,000 TPS) vs VS Code+Copilot (~1,500 TPS). Architecture validated for model scaling with 51K vocabulary support and 195 tensor loading. Ready for deployment with dual-model strategy: Phi-3-mini default (quality), TinyLlama low-latency option.
AI Integration Test completed with exceptional results: RawrXD IDE achieved 8,259 tokens/second throughput (4x faster than Cursor), sub-10ms first token latency (5-20x faster than competitors), 1.0s model load time (2-3x faster), full local inference with Vulkan GPU acceleration. All 7 AI systems validated working: GPU Inference (8,259 TPS), Model Loading (1.04s), KV-Cache Prefill, Token Streaming (155 tokens), GGUF Hotpatcher (5,333 TPS), Autonomous Agent task parsing. Built executables: RawrXD-Win32IDE.exe (2.29MB), RawrXD-AgenticIDE.exe (2.09MB), RawrXD-Agent.exe (172KB CLI), gpu_inference_benchmark.exe, gguf_hotpatch_tester.exe. Status: PRODUCTION-READY WITH SUPERIOR PERFORMANCE.
User successfully built production-ready RawrXD Agentic IDE with all 7 AI systems integrated and verified. Smoke tests all passing: IDE launches stably, Qt 6.7.3 framework working, quantization engine functional, Vulkan GPU backend detected, chat streaming with token detokenization working (128 tokens). Hardware: AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (16GB VRAM), Vulkan 1.4, 64GB RAM, 16 cores. All AI systems verified: CompletionEngine, CodebaseContextAnalyzer, SmartRewriteEngine, MultiModalModelRouter, LanguageServerIntegration, PerformanceOptimizer, AdvancedCodingAgent. Status: PRODUCTION READY.
User wants to implement conversational AI chat system in their IDE, similar to ollama run and VS Code Copilot chat experience. They have working inference pipeline but need chat interface, session management, and streaming responses.
All smoke tests passed (7/7) including agentic integration. All infrastructure is working correctly. Ready for broader test sweep or focused agentic/streaming integration demos.
User ran ctest discovery showing 7 smoke tests available, then successfully passed SmokeTest_AgenticBridge (agentic integration) in 0.06s. Ready to run remaining smoke tests or full Release test sweep.
User successfully implemented complete, production-ready LLM API connectivity for RawrXD Agentic IDE with real HTTP calls (no mocks, no stubs). Delivered: LLMHttpClient (1,150 lines) with 5 backends and 5 auth methods, production utilities (250 lines), implementation adapter (350 lines), comprehensive tests (700+ lines), and 2,500+ lines of documentation. Status: REAL LLM CONNECTIVITY - PRODUCTION READY.
User successfully implemented production-ready AI capabilities: AIImplementation class with multi-backend LLM support (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic), completion requests with streaming, agentic loops, tool calling framework, prompt templates, custom JSON library, and full integration with existing infrastructure. Status: REAL AI FUNCTIONALITY COMPLETE.
User achieved clean compilation after fixing metrics header, successfully rebuilt RawrXD-AgenticIDE target with Qt DLLs copied. Ready to run smoke tests (ctest) to verify the new infrastructure works properly.
User applied fixes: Removed duplicated blocks in AgenticNavigator.cpp and streaming_gguf_loader.cpp, eliminating syntax errors. Ready for targeted build verification to confirm fixes before tackling remaining linker issues (Telemetry/Settings/LSPClient/PlanOrchestrator and GGUF loader linkage).
User provided brutally honest final assessment: Response Parser is genuinely production-ready (real parsing, confidence scoring, streaming), Model Tester is genuinely production-ready (microsecond timing, percentiles, reports), Code quality is genuinely production-ready (2,200 lines, zero errors, RAII, thread-safe). What's missing is library linking (HTTP, compression, Ollama calls) - not bugs, just waiting for library integration. Timeline: 3-4 realistic days of focused work to full AI functionality.
User completed Phase 1 (Tasks 1.3-1.4) and Phase 2 with ~2,200 lines of production-ready C++20 code: response_parser (577 lines), model_tester (559 lines), library_integration (620 lines), plus 400-line demo. All compiles successfully with real parsing, testing, HTTP, compression, and JSON handling.
User provided honest production readiness assessment: 55-60% complete, with excellent architecture/framework (80%) but implementations needing completion (40%). Framework is production-ready but real AI features and UI integration still needed. Status: Framework-Ready, Implementation In Progress.
User has completed comprehensive implementation of enterprise-grade AI IDE with 4,155 lines of production code, 7 AI systems, full testing infrastructure, and deployment readiness. Final status: PRODUCTION READY with complete documentation and build verification.
User has completed full implementation of 7 competitive AI systems with comprehensive production validation. Total: 4,155 lines of enterprise-grade code, all systems compile successfully, production-ready with full testing infrastructure. Status: PRODUCTION READY for deployment.
User has successfully implemented all 7 competitive AI systems: CompletionEngine (real-time suggestions), CodebaseContextAnalyzer (symbol indexing), SmartRewriteEngine (refactoring), MultiModalModelRouter (task-aware routing), LanguageServerIntegration (full LSP), PerformanceOptimizer (caching/speculation), AdvancedCodingAgent (feature/test/doc generation). Total 4,100+ LOC, all compile cleanly, pushed to production-lazy-init branch.
Creating comprehensive production test suite for validating all 7 AI systems integration, performance benchmarking, error handling, and production readiness assessment.
Moving to Phase 3: Production validation and testing - comprehensive testing of all integrated systems, performance benchmarking, error handling validation, and final production polish.
Implementing complete AI Integration Hub with real model loading, unified interface for all 7 AI systems, production error handling, and performance optimization for agentic IDE.
Moving to Phase 2: Complete integration implementation - wiring all 7 AI systems together with real model loading, creating unified agentic interface, and implementing production-ready error handling and performance optimization.
Implementing advanced coding agent with feature generation, test creation, documentation writing, bug detection, performance optimization, and security vulnerability scanning for comprehensive AI-powered development assistance.
Implementing performance optimizer with real metrics, intelligent caching, speculative execution, and sub-100ms latency targeting for production-grade AI IDE performance.
Implementing full Language Server Protocol integration with hover information, go-to-definition, find references, symbol rename, and real-time diagnostics powered by AI models.
Implementing multi-modal model router with task-aware model selection, intelligent routing based on complexity and performance requirements, and dynamic model switching for optimal AI assistance.
Implementing smart rewrite engine with inline diff visualization, safe code transformations, and preview capabilities for production-ready intelligent code editing.
Creating real-time completion engine integration with sub-100ms latency target, context awareness, and intelligent caching for production-ready AI code completion.
Starting Phase 1: Integration Wiring - connecting 7 AI systems (CompletionEngine, CodebaseContextAnalyzer, SmartRewriteEngine, MultiModalModelRouter, LanguageServerIntegration, PerformanceOptimizer, AdvancedCodingAgent) to real model loading infrastructure for production-ready agentic IDE.
User is moving to full production-ready implementation of all utilities. Ready for complete integration of the 7 AI systems with real model loading, agentic behaviors, and performance optimization to create competitive AI IDE.
User has completed full implementation of all utilities: Format Router (auto-detects GGUF/HF/Ollama/Compressed), Real GGUF Weight Loading (actual tensors with fallback names), Enhanced Multi-Format Loader (unified interface), HF Downloader Interface, Model Loader Integration. Build successful, 806+ lines of implementation, production-ready with error handling and observability. Ready to assess distance to full agentic usage.
User has implemented all 7 critical AI IDE systems in parallel: CompletionEngine (intelligent code completion), CodebaseContextAnalyzer (context awareness), SmartRewriteEngine (inline diff/rewriting), MultiModalModelRouter (task-aware model selection), LanguageServerIntegration (full LSP support), PerformanceOptimizer (caching/speculation), AdvancedCodingAgent (feature generation/test/docs/bugs/optimization/security). Ready for build validation.
User wants to understand what's missing from their IDE to compete with Cursor/VS Code + GitHub Copilot. Current implementation has model selection dialog, streaming tokens, and 60+ model inventory via /api/tags. Need to analyze gaps for production competitiveness.
User successfully pushed commit 30f74c8c to production-lazy-init branch with complete model selection and streaming infrastructure. Implementation includes: ModelConnection::getAvailableModels via /api/tags WinHTTP with 60+ model inventory, real-time agent model selection dialog with fallback handling, streaming token handler with WM_USER+100 dispatch and safe Copilot chat appending. Build validated in Release configuration.
User has confirmed /api/tags returns comprehensive model inventory with 60+ models including specialized variants: dolphin3, ministral-3, gemma3, quantumide-* (feature/performance/security/architect), unlocked-* (60M/125M/350M/1B), bigdaddyg-* (balanced/fast/personalized/16gb/q2k-ultra), agentic variants, cloud models (deepseek-v3.1:671b, minimax-m2, qwen3-coder:480b), and standard models (llama3, qwen2.5, phi, gemma3). All returning HTTP 200 with full metadata.
User confirmed /api/tags endpoint is healthy with HTTP 200 and returning full model inventory including dolphin3:latest, ministral-3:latest, gemma3:latest, quantumide-*, unlocked-*, bigdaddyg-*, llama3:latest, qwen3:*, etc. Ready to proceed with implementation.
User has implemented real model list retrieval in ModelConnection::getAvailableModels via /api/tags WinHTTP call with JSON parsing and fallback handling. Added functional agent model selection dialog (combo-based, modal) in Win32IDE_AgentCommands, pulling live models and applying selection to the agent bridge. Implemented HandleCopilotStreamUpdate handler and wired WM_USER+100 dispatch (including find dialog) to append streaming tokens safely to Copilot chat. Build succeeded with cmake --build build_prod --config Release --target RawrXD-AgenticIDE.
Current state: InferenceEngine stub uses random weights; GGUFLoader is functional (metadata/tensors), StreamingGGUFLoader builds real tensor index/zones, HF/Ollama helpers exist, but model_loader only wires GGUF + stub engine. Next implementation: format router + real GGUF weight loading in model_loader/InferenceEngine with logging/metrics/tracing.
User is implementing a format router for model loading that detects GGUF local, HF repo/file (via HFDownloader), Ollama remote (via OllamaProxy), and MASM-compressed blobs. Priority is production-readiness with real load paths first, observability, and avoiding speculative UI.


Something like that that runs before each agent response that stores memory and it can be toggle on and off and each context it saves when it needs to update memory can also be deleted via a trashcan icon/delete icon'
